methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Panthalassa is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Garth Sheldon-Coulson. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company operating in the same sector. The three most directly comparable peers—Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy—are all Series A stage companies in climate and energy.

Given the limited number of applicable valuation methodologies for a Series A company with undisclosed financing history, peer-median analysis forms the primary basis for triangulation. The median valuation across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables is $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This peer-median approach yields an implied valuation of $150M for Panthalassa. The broader comparable universe includes later-stage private companies such as TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials, as well as the public company LNZA, though these are less directly applicable given Panthalassa's stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on a median of $150M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty in relying on a single primary methodology and a limited peer set of three companies. Key uncertainties include the specific capital efficiency and go-to-market traction of Panthalassa relative to its comparables, neither of which is quantified in available data. The range should be treated as indicative pending disclosure of company-specific operational metrics.
